title: XPeng Stock Down 13.9% After Record Deliveries and Margin Milestone
text: XPeng (XPEV) experienced a 13.9% stock price decline following its Q3 earnings announcement on November 17, 2025, despite delivering impressive operational results. The company reported record quarterly vehicle deliveries of 116,007 units and achieved a significant milestone by reaching a gross margin above 20% for the first time. Net losses narrowed substantially compared to the prior year. XPeng provided Q4 2025 guidance expecting between 125,000 to 132,000 vehicle deliveries, but this outlook arrived below market consensus expectations, triggering the stock's decline. Despite strong delivery numbers and margin improvement, analysts note investors must balance enthusiasm about operational momentum against persistent risks. The company projects substantial growth through 2028, though it currently operates at a loss. One analyst valued the stock at approximately $26.95, suggesting 20% upside potential, though community valuations range from $9.86 to $33.26. The core tension remains whether XPeng can achieve profitability while navigating intense competitive pressures in the electric vehicle market.
